DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES; MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION IS DENIED. MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IS NOT OBLIGATED TO PROVIDE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE IN POSSESSION OF 3RD PARTIES (E.G. BUREAU OF PRISONS). THEREFORE, ANY POLICIES AND PROCEDURES OF THE BUREAU OF PRISONS MUST BE OBTAINED FROM THAT ENTITY. HOWEVER, IF THE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PROVIDED, OR HAVE IN THEIR POSSESSION, ANY POLICIES, PROCEDURES OR REGULATIONS OF THE BUREAU OF PRISONS IN THEIR POSSESSION, THEY MUST PROVIDE A COPY OF THOSE DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF A COPY OF ANY CONTRACT BETWEEN ITSELF AND THE BUREAU OF PRISONS OR CORNELL COMPANIES AND BUREAU OF PRISONS FOR THE TIME PERIOD PLAINTIFF WAS AT THE DEFENDANT'S FACILITY. DEFENDANT WILL PROVIDE A LIST OF ANY LAW SUITS OR CRIMINAL ACTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS STEMMING FROM ACTIVITIES AT ANY HALFWAY HOUSES FROM 2 YEARS PRIOR TO, AND ONE YEAR FOLLOWING PLAINTIFF'S STAY AT DEFENDANT'S FACILITY. THE COURT ADOPTS THE REMAINDER OF ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES GRANTED EXCEPT AS TO 2.3, 2.4, 11.1 AND 11.2. RESPONSE MUST BE PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF BY 3/18/11 AT 5:00PM. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI

REQ. FOR JUDICIAL NTC IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S OPPOS. TO PLTF'S MTN FOR JUDGMENT ON PLEADINGS; MTN TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CORNELL CORRECTIONS OF CALIFORNIA INC. CORNELL COMPANIES INC. RICHARD, MARIA REED, MIKE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MOTION TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT PROPERLY VERIFIED. EVEN IF IT WERE, THE PROPER WAY TO CHALLENGE AN UNVERIFIED ANSWER IS THROUGH A MOTION TO STRIKE, NOT A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PERLMAN V. MUNICIPAL CRT (1979) 99 CAL.APP.3D 568, 575. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED THIS ISSUE BY NOT RAISING IT FOR OVER A YEAR. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

calendar.matters:
Mtn To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG's Motion To Tax Costs is Denied. Defendants are entitled to recover copying costs of $ 539.75 for the clerk's transcript. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(B).) Defendants did not waive the copying costs in writing. Defendants' emails contemplate the shipping costs associated with borrowing the transcript only. Printing and copying costs of $ 0.25 per page is reasonable. Therefore, Defendants are also entitled to recover printing costs of $ 310.00 for briefs. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(E).) Defendants are entitled to recover costs of $ 57.62 for overnight delivery. (CRC 8.278(d)(1)(D).) (The Court will sign the proposed order submitted by email prior to the hearing.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion Fo Rjudgment On The Pleadings; Motion To Deny Summary Judgment Motion
Off Calendar Jul-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MOTION TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION.

APPEARANCES: Jordan Rosenberg, 206/666-6517 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Nicholas C. Miller, Esq. of McCormick, Barstow, Sheppard, Wayte & Carruth LLP 559/433-1300 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT PROPERLY VERIFIED. EVEN IF IT WERE, THE PROPER WAY TO CHALLENGE AN UNVERIFIED ANSWER IS THROUGH A MOTION TO STRIKE, NOT A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PERLMAN V. MUNICIPAL CRT (1979) 99 CAL.APP.3D 568, 575. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED THIS ISSUE BY NOT RAISING IT FOR OVER A YEAR. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Ntc Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S MOTION TO COMPEL - DUPLICATE ENTRY. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi;

Ntc And Motio Top Compel Discovery Responses ; Motion For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES; MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Jordan Rosenberg, 206/660-6517 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Nicholas C. Miller, Esq. of Wayte & Carruth LLP 559/433-1300 for defts & opposing parties (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as to the following:
MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION IS DENIED. MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED.
In addition, the Court took the matter under submission as to the MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows:
Defendant is not obligated to provide documents that are in possession of 3rd parties (e.g. Bureau of Prisons). Therefore, any policies and procedures of the Bureau of Prisons must be obtained from that entity. However, if the documents have been provided, or have in their possession, any policies, procedures or regulations of the Bureau of Prisons in their possession, they must provide a copy of those documents to plaintiff.
Defendant must provide to plaintiff a copy of any contract between itself and the Bureau of Prisons or Cornell Companies and Bureau of Prisons for the time period plaintiff was at the defendant's facility.
Defendant will provide a list of any law suits or criminal actions against defendants stemming from activities at any halfway houses from 2 years prior to, and one year following plaintiff's stay at defendant's facility.
The Court adopts the remainder of its tentative ruling: MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES GRANTED EXCEPT AS TO 2.3, 2.4, 11.1 AND 11.2.
Response must be provided to plaintiff by 3/18/11 at 5:00pm.
Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi;
